question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a specially crafted account alias (e.g., very long string) as idOrAliasOrEvmAddress to cause EntityService.getEncodedId() to perform an expensive string operation, consuming CPU cycles needed for gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply many spender.id= parameters with the gt operator to generate multiple range conditions that are ANDed together, creating a logically impossible query that still causes the DB to perform a full scan before returning empty results, wasting resources needed for gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link generation (utils.getPaginationLink()) to craft a next link that encodes a spender.id value causing an out-of-bounds parameter index in the SQL query, leading to a DB error that disrupts gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a value equal to the ownerCondition account ID to create a self-referential allowance query that causes unexpected DB behavior, potentially interfering with gossip transaction validation for that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an order parameter set to an unexpected value not in orderFilterValues to bypass the order validation and inject a malformed ORDER BY clause into the SQL query, causing a DB error that blocks gossip transaction writes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a negative spender.id value to bypass the isPositiveLong validation in utils.buildAndValidateFilters(), causing an invalid SQL parameter that triggers a DB error and disrupts the gossip transaction ingestion pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async nature of getAccountCryptoAllowances() to send a burst of requests that all await EntityService.getEncodedId() simultaneously, exhausting the DB connection pool and preventing gossip transactions from being recorded?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a value of 9223372036854775807 (MAX_LONG) combined with a gte operator to create a condition that matches only the maximum possible spender, causing the DB to perform an index scan to the end of the table and wasting I/O needed for gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the response.links.next pagination to traverse all allowances for a target account without any rate limiting, accumulating complete knowledge of all spender relationships to map the gossip transaction network topology?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a non-numeric string that passes initial validation but fails at the DB parameter binding stage, causing an unhandled DB error that crashes the request handler and drops pending gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the amountCondition (amount > 0) static filter to infer which allowances have been fully consumed (amount = 0), and use that information to identify accounts whose gossip transactions are likely to fail authorization, enabling targeted suppression?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a value that is a valid Hedera entity ID in shard.realm.num format to bypass numeric validation and cause EntityService resolution to perform an additional DB lookup, amplifying the resource cost per request and degrading gossip throughput?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of request body size limits on the query string to supply an extremely long query string with many spender.id parameters, causing the HTTP parser to consume excessive memory and preventing gossip transaction HTTP callbacks from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit=0 parameter to cause the SQL LIMIT clause to be set to 0, returning no results but still executing the full WHERE clause evaluation, wasting DB resources needed for gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CryptoAllowanceService.getAccountAllowancesQuery() method's string concatenation of whereConditions to inject a SQL comment (--) via a crafted filter value, truncating the amountCondition and returning zero-amount allowances that reveal suppressed gossip transaction authorizations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a Unicode or multi-byte character sequence that passes JavaScript string validation but causes the DB driver to misinterpret the parameter, leading to a query error that disrupts gossip transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ownerCondition uses a static string (owner = $1) to send requests for accounts that do not exist, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to return null or throw, and if not properly handled, causing an unhandled promise rejection that crashes the gossip transaction handler?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a value of 1 combined with a lt operator to create a condition that matches no valid spenders (since IDs start at 1), causing the DB to perform an unnecessary index scan and wasting resources needed for gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the response serialization of large allowance arrays (allowances.map()) to cause JSON serialization of BigInt values to fail or produce incorrect output, leading to a response error that disrupts the client's ability to track gossip transaction allowances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple order= parameters in the same request to cause the last value to override previous ones without validation, potentially setting an unexpected order that causes the DB to use a different index and degrade gossip transaction write performance?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link (response.links.next) to replay a previous request with a stale spender.id cursor value, causing the DB to return inconsistent results that mislead the attacker about which gossip transactions are authorized?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a value that is a valid EVM address (0x-prefixed 20-byte hex) to trigger a different code path in EntityService.getEncodedId(), causing an additional DB lookup that amplifies the per-request DB cost and degrades gossip transaction throughput?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async/await chain in getAccountCryptoAllowances() to cause a race condition where a concurrent allowance update (from a gossip transaction) is read mid-write, returning stale allowance data that causes the attacker to incorrectly assess which transactions will be gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a value of 0.0.0 (shard.realm.num format with zero num) to cause EntityService resolution to return account ID 0, which may match system accounts and expose their allowance relationships used in gossip transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum query string length check to send a request with thousands of spender.id= parameters, causing the Node.js HTTP parser to allocate a large buffer and exhaust heap memory, preventing gossip transaction event processing?
